ocr.text: In the evaluation of predictive accuracy across various models, the table presents a comprehensive comparison of three distinct
modeling approaches: Model 1, Model 2, and Model 3. Each model is assessed based on critical metrics, including the Mean
Absolute Percentage Error of the sum (MAPSEsum) and the Cumulative Average Monthly Mean Forecasting Score
(CAMMFS).
Parameters and statistics  Model 1  Model 2  Model 3
Predictor  MAPSEsum  CAMMFS  LWFS
MSE (dispersion)  9.34  7.98  5.95
GoF test* (P value)
Δdeviance (χ2 test)  <0.001  <0.001  <0.001
The MAPSEsum serves as a crucial indicator of the models' overall performance, highlighting the percentage deviation of
predicted values from actual observations, thereby allowing for a straightforward interpretation of forecasting accuracy.
Conversely, the CAMMFS provides insights into the models' effectiveness over time, evaluating their ability to consistently
deliver reliable forecasts on a monthly basis. By analyzing these metrics, stakeholders can discern the relative strengths and
weaknesses of each model, facilitating informed decisions regarding their application in real-world scenarios. Furthermore, the
inclusion of additional parameters, such as the Last Week Forecast Score (LWFS), enhances the robustness of this comparative
analysis, offering a multidimensional view of predictive capabilities. This data-driven approach underscores the importance of
tailored modeling strategies in achieving optimal forecasting outcomes in complex environments.
